subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the character traits of Dr. Dolittle that make him a unique protagonist
Identify and interpret the specific ways animals communicate in the story
Understand the transition in Dr. Dolittle’s career and the social consequences thereof
Explore the theme of specialized knowledge and its benefits to society
Develop descriptive and narrative writing skills using animal-related vocabulary
Differentiate between literal and figurative language in the context of animal 'speech'

lesson_plan.title: Character Study of Dr. Dolittle
topics_covered: Contrast between Dr. Dolittle and 'normal' doctors
The impact of his pets on his social standing
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis: Human doctoring vs. Animal doctoring
Discussion on social eccentricity and its consequences
student_activities: Create a 'Character Map' for Dr. Dolittle (Traits, Challenges, Desires)
Identify 3 reasons why his human patients stopped visiting

lesson_plan.title: The Science of Animal Communication
topics_covered: The concept of 'signs' and 'signals' in nature
teaching_strategy: Reading the dialogue between Polynesia and the Doctor
Explaining the logic behind the parrot’s argument
student_activities: Group work: Invent 'animal signs' for common phrases like 'I'm thirsty' or 'Watch out!'
Annotate the text for examples of how the parrot explains 'Bird ABCs'

lesson_plan.title: Career Pivot and Conflict
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the 'turning point' in a narrative
Analyzing the pros and cons of the Doctor’s career change
student_activities: Write a short 'Pros and Cons' list for Dr. Dolittle’s new career
Debate: Was the Doctor being responsible or reckless?

lesson_plan.title: Narrative Perspective: The Animal View
topics_covered: How the news of the Animal Doctor spreads
The reputation of Dr. Dolittle among birds and beasts
Imagery in the 'animal rush' to the surgery
teaching_strategy: Reading the section on the Doctor’s growing fame
Visualizing the 'animal waiting room' through text descriptions
Focusing on the use of vivid verbs and adjectives
student_activities: Write a short paragraph from Jip the dog’s perspective about the new patients
Draw the waiting room based strictly on the sensory details provided in the text

lesson_plan.title: Themes of Empathy and Expertise
topics_covered: The moral responsibility of having a unique skill
Empathy as a tool for problem-solving
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on why the animals trusted the Doctor more than others
Relating 'animal language' to modern-day environmental stewardship
student_activities: Create a 'Code of Conduct' for an animal doctor
Write the 'Moral of the Story' in their own words, backed by 2 examples

lesson_plan.title: Language Workshop and Quiz
topics_covered: Identifying Nouns and Adjectives in pet descriptions
teaching_strategy: Grammar game: Identifying descriptive adjectives in the text
Introduction to animal-related idioms (e.g., 'To let the cat out of the bag')
student_activities: Match animal idioms with their meanings

teaching_aids: Video clip of a parrot imitating sounds (real-world connection)
teacher_tip: Focus on the transition from 'hearing' to 'listening.' Use Polynesia as a model for a mentor. Encourage students to think about how specialized knowledge (like animal language) can change a person's role in society.
